title: Cozy Jamaican Vegetable Stew
description: A tangy and molten Jamaican dish featuring tortillas, cooked in a cataplana vessel with a blend of spices and plant-based ingredients, inspired by traditional ttukbaegi-earthenware-cook methods.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cut the tortillas into quarters and set aside.
Heat a large cataplana vessel over medium heat and add the chopped onion, cooking until softened.
Add the minced garlic and diced scotch bonnet peppers, cooking for an additional minute.
Add the cubed sweet potatoes and sliced carrots, cooking for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the coconut milk, jamaican spice blend, and vegetable broth, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt to taste and stir in the quartered tortillas.
Transfer the cataplana vessel to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes, or until the tortillas are crispy and the stew is molten.
